paper_title: Late Holocene Transformations of the Central Po Plain

methods.description: This method involves creating a Digital Terrain Model (DTM) of the research area using geomorphological literature, high-resolution LiDAR data, and QGIS software to analyze the Late Holocene transformations of the Central Po Plain.

steps.description: The geomorphological literature on the Central Po Valley constituted a useful starting point in association with a 1-m DEM (produced from high-resolution LiDAR) provided by the national geodatabase.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch 1-m DEM data from national geodatabase

steps.description: The elevation checkpoints (m.a.s.l.) provided by the regional geodatabase have been elaborated with the software QGIS (2.18) to draw contour lines with an equidistance of 0.5 m and to create the final Digital Terrain Model (DTM) of the research area.
steps.searchable_summary: process elevation data to create DTM using QGIS

steps.description: A 3D model has been created by the software QGIS plugin Qgis2threejs exaggerating the DTM Z-value ×50.
steps.searchable_summary: create 3D model from DTM using Qgis2threejs

methods.description: This method integrates archaeological and historical data with geomorphological analysis to detect and contextualize landforms and human activities in the Central Po Plain during the Late Holocene.

steps.description: The archaeological evidence (sites and materials) has been compiled from various records, a regional web database, and terrain surveys.
steps.searchable_summary: compile archaeological data from records and surveys

steps.description: The 18th and 19th century transcriptions of medieval accounts and parchments served as an advantageous starting point in the analysis of the historical documents along with the 20th-century editions of medieval documents kept at the Parma and Reggio Emilia National Historical Archives.
All of the archaeological (sites and materials) and historical (place names) data have been organized according to epochs in different layers using QGIS software.
steps.searchable_summary: organize data into layers by epoch using QGIS
